array-assets.name: Array Assets
array-assets.description: A visual design reference sheet for an educational math array tool, showcasing four array representations (Expanded Array, Condensed Array, Egg Carton, and Muffin Tin) each displayed in three interactive states: Default, Rows Highlighted, and Columns Highlighted. The highlighting states use a glowing blue wash effect to emphasize individual rows or columns, supporting students' understanding of multiplication groupings.

frame-528.name: Frame 528
frame-528.description: An incremental array-building toy showing a **rows-only construction mode**, where students add one row at a time to build a rectangular array. The current visual state appears to be at an early scaffold level, guiding Grade 3 students to understand multiplication as repeated groups of equal rows before introducing columns.

frame-529.name: Frame 529
frame-529.description: An incremental array builder toy displaying items arranged in a single row (one row of multiple items), representing the early scaffold level where only per-row quantity is being established. The UI appears to be in a row-only mode, limiting array construction to a single horizontal grouping without yet introducing multiple rows.

details.name: Details
details.description: A text-based documentation frame describing three array problem interaction modes for a Grade 3 math toy: **Drag & Drop Two Statements**, **Equation Builder with Static Visual**, and **Incremental Array Builder**. The Incremental Array Builder is described in detail, featuring row/column increment controls with conditional visual states (highlights vs. dots) and a toggleable total dot counter for assisted learning.

frame-475.name: Frame 475
frame-475.description: The image only shows the text label **"Array Styles"** — likely a section header or title frame — with no interactive toy content, visual state, or UI elements visible beyond the bold typography.

frame-501.name: Frame 501
frame-501.description: The image only shows the text "**Highlights**" — a section header label — with no additional UI elements, interactive components, or visual content visible in this frame.

interactive-construction-toy.name: Interactive Construction Toy
interactive-construction-toy.description: An interactive multiplication array activity showing a **3-row × 10-column dot grid** (30 dots total), asking students to identify the **rotated expression** (e.g., switching rows and columns) by filling in a **"___ × ___" input** using a number selector (1–10) at the bottom.

word-selection-&-build-expression.name: Word Selection & Build Expression
word-selection-&-build-expression.description: A drag-and-drop math expression builder where Grade 3 students identify both commutative multiplication expressions for a **4×5 dot array**, with one expression already scaffolded (4×5 OR 5×4) and a number bank (1–10) available below for selection, pending a "Check" submission.

rotate-then-drag-&-drop.name: Rotate then Drag & Drop
rotate-then-drag-&-drop.description: A digital math learning activity showing a **10×3 array** of blue dots arranged vertically, prompting Grade 3 students to rotate the array using clickable arrows to explore the commutative property of multiplication. Key UI elements include a virtual avatar/tutor, a "Repeat" audio button, rotation arrows flanking the array, and a disabled "Check" button awaiting interaction.

array-builder.name: Array Builder
array-builder.description: An interactive array-building activity where a student must arrange 4 rows of 6 items, currently showing 1 row of 6 (Total = 6) with stepper controls for adjusting Rows and Items Per Row. The UI includes an animated avatar guide, the partially-built array displayed as blue vertical bars, and a "Check" button to validate the answer.

prototype:-rotating-arrays-visualization.name: PROTOTYPE: Rotating Arrays Visualization
prototype:-rotating-arrays-visualization.description: A digital interactive math lesson showing a **4×5 dot array** (4 rows of 5) to teach multiplication, with a 3D animated tutor character explaining "Using Rows: 4 rows of 5 = 4 × 5." The UI includes a top navigation bar with **Repeat** and **Next** buttons, and the array is displayed as 20 blue dots arranged in a grid on a light blue background.

rotating-arrays-visualization.name: Rotating Arrays Visualization
rotating-arrays-visualization.description: An interactive digital math lesson displays a 4×5 dot array (4 rows, 5 columns = 20 dots) with rotation arrows on either side, prompting students to click to rotate the array and observe the commutative property of multiplication. Key UI elements include a 3D animated teacher avatar, a repeat audio button, instructional text at the top, and a "Next" navigation button.

prototype:-word-selection-&-build-expres.description: A digital math learning activity showing a **4×4 dot array** (16 dots arranged in equal rows and columns) where Grade 3 students must select words ("Rows" or "Columns") to construct a multiplication expression describing the array. The interface is in an **initial/unstarted state** with no words yet chosen, featuring a virtual avatar guide, a "Repeat" audio button, and disabled "Next" and "Check" buttons awaiting student input.

prototype:-array-builder.name: PROTOTYPE: Array Builder
prototype:-array-builder.description: An interactive array-builder math activity where Grade 3 students drag number tiles (1–10) into two input boxes to form a multiplication expression (□ × □) that equals exactly 20 total items. The interface is in its initial/empty state with no values selected yet, featuring a 3D avatar tutor, a repeat audio button, and a greyed-out "Check" button awaiting valid input.

The current visual state shows a 4-column × 5-row dot array nearly complete, with a number tile selector (1–10) at the bottom for inputting values, and a "Check" button to validate the answer.

prototype:-double-array-builder,-same-sh.name: PROTOTYPE: Double Array Builder, Same Shape
prototype:-double-array-builder,-same-sh.description: A Grade 3 math activity where students build a second array equivalent to 3×10 (20 items) using a drag-and-drop interface, with the left array showing a completed 3×10 arrangement and the right showing a partially built 10×3 rotated array. Key UI elements include a number card bank (1–10), prefilled equation slots showing "10 × 3," and a confirm checkmark button, scaffolded to guide students toward understanding the commutative property of multiplication.

frame-513.name: Frame 513
frame-513.description: A "Double Array Builder" activity showing two arrays that share the same shape/dimensions, designed to help Grade 3 students explore how two different multiplication equations can describe the same rectangular arrangement. The current visual state appears to be an empty or initial scaffold with no groups or items populated yet, awaiting student interaction to build and compare the two arrays.

multi-select.name: Multi Select
multi-select.description: A multi-select question screen from an interactive math learning app asks Grade 3 students to identify which picture represents 2×6 (2 groups of 6), with one answer already checked (bottom-left, showing 2 rows of 6 dots). The UI features four answer choices with checkboxes, an audio "Repeat" button restating the concept, a virtual avatar teacher, and a "Check" button to validate the selection.

prototype:-rotate-then-drag-&-drop.name: PROTOTYPE: Rotate then Drag & Drop
prototype:-rotate-then-drag-&-drop.description: A digital math learning activity where Grade 3 students interact with a **10×3 dot array** that can be rotated using arrow controls, demonstrating the commutative property of multiplication. The current state shows **30 dots arranged in 3 columns of 10 rows**, with a 3D avatar tutor, a "Repeat" audio button, and a greyed-out "Check" button awaiting student interaction.

compare-two-arrays.name: Compare Two Arrays
compare-two-arrays.description: A voice-guided multiple-choice quiz interaction where Grade 3 students apply the commutative property of multiplication by identifying that **7 × 6 = 42**, given three reference facts as scaffolding. The student has selected "42" (highlighted in blue) with the **Check** button available, and a 3D avatar provides audio support via the **Repeat** button.

double-expression-builder.name: Double Expression Builder
double-expression-builder.description: A drag-and-drop math activity where Grade 3 students fill in blank tiles to describe a **6×10 dot array** using two complementary statements (rows and columns), with both blanks already correctly filled showing "6 rows of 10 items" and "10 columns of 6 items." A numbered tile bank (1–10) sits at the bottom, and a "Check" button is available to validate the answers.

expression-builder.name: Expression Builder
expression-builder.description: A digital math activity where Grade 3 students build a multiplication equation from a dot array (2 rows × 7 dots = 14) by selecting number tiles from a bottom tray. The current state shows an unanswered row-based equation scaffold (□ × □ = □) with number tiles 2–14 available, and an avatar instructing students to match words to expressions.
